escalation.rationale: Simultaneous active US-Iran war with Trump threatening strikes on nuclear sites, Houthi maritime blockade closing both Red Sea chokepoints, Ukraine military leadership overhaul amid protests, and North Korea reaffirming combat support for Russia collectively push conditions to High escalation.

ticker_items: Ukraine names Drapatyi new commander after Zelenskyy sacks Syrskyi amid protests.
Trump threatens imminent strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site.
Houthis declare Saudi naval blockade; tankers reversing at Bab el-Mandeb.
Brent crude hits ~$94/barrel as dual Red Sea and Hormuz chokepoints threatened.
Russia issues arrest warrant for Ukraine's newly appointed military chief Drapatyi.

new_entrant.text: Iraq's PM made a first official visit to Iran on 2026-07-23 signaling deepening Baghdad-Tehran alignment as the US-Iran war continues, though Iraq has not formally entered hostilities.

eastern_europe.description: Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones at the Moscow region overnight, wounding two people and setting several facilities ablaze.
Ukrainian military struck Wildberries retailer warehouses in the third such attack in a week, part of Kyiv's expanding long-range retaliatory strike campaign.
Ukraine employed FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles with salvaged engines to strike targets deep inside Russian territory for the first time.
Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones targeting the Moscow region overnight, wounding two people and setting multiple facilities ablaze.

middle_east.description: Iran launched missiles and drones in a stepped-up attack wave; Bahrain sirens sounded as two waves were intercepted.
Jordan intercepted Iranian missile and drone waves as Iran escalated its regional attack campaign.
Houthi rebels declared a maritime blockade of Saudi ports and threatened shipping transiting Bab al-Mandeb, causing ships to divert.
US and Israeli attacks on Iran continued as part of the ongoing war, with ceasefire talks ongoing in Pakistan.
Iran launched missiles and drones toward Bahrain, with sirens sounding and Bahrain reporting intercept of two waves.
Jordan announced interception of Iranian missile and drone waves as Iran stepped up regional attacks two weeks after war with US resumed.

middle_east.target_location: Bab el-Mandeb Strait / Southern Red Sea
middle_east.description: Houthis deployed missiles and drones against ships in the southern Red Sea enforcing a declared embargo on Saudi crude exports through Bab el-Mandeb.
Saudi Arabia-backed strikes hit Sana'a Airport, prompting Houthis to vow an 'eye for an eye' retaliatory response.

today.kia: ~3,468 in Iran; ~3,696 in Lebanon; ~28 in Gulf states

today.incident: US-Israel attacks on Gulf states (cumulative tracker)

claimed_totals.figure: est. 1,600 casualties in single day (Fark thread claim)

claimed_totals.claim: Iran, Lebanon, and Gulf state deaths from US-Israel attacks

russia.text: Sumy and Kharkiv border areas will not be returned in any peace deal.
russia.speaker: Kremlin spokesperson
russia.title: Kremlin

russia.text: Russia thanks Pyongyang for supporting its war against Ukraine.
russia.speaker: Vladimir Putin
russia.title: President of Russia

russia.text: US weapons, intelligence, and Starlink support are helping guide Ukrainian attacks on targets inside Russia, including civilian sites.
russia.speaker: Sergey Lavrov
russia.title: Russian Foreign Minister

russia.text: Lavrov discussed Ukraine peace efforts, Europe's militarization, the Iran war, nuclear proliferation and Russia's ties with ASEAN.
russia.speaker: Sergey Lavrov
russia.title: Russian Foreign Minister

ukraine.text: I have been tasked with stepping up counteroffensive actions.
ukraine.speaker: Mykhailo Drapatyi
ukraine.title: Commander-in-Chief, Ukrainian Armed Forces

ukraine.text: Zelenskyy dismissed Defense Minister Fedorov and Gen. Syrskyi amid military tensions and nationwide protests over strategic direction.
ukraine.speaker: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
ukraine.title: President of Ukraine

middle_east.text: Iran is the troublemaker of the Gulf region and any Houthi attempt to block Red Sea shipping would be problematic.
middle_east.speaker: Marco Rubio
middle_east.title: US Secretary of State

middle_east.text: Lebanon is working towards a complete Israeli withdrawal from areas it occupies.
middle_east.speaker: Nawaf Salam
middle_east.title: Prime Minister of Lebanon

middle_east.text: I narrowly escaped the Israeli assassination strike at the opening of the war.
middle_east.speaker: Abbas Araghchi
middle_east.title: Iranian Foreign Minister

middle_east.text: The conflict is now a full-scale war.
middle_east.speaker: Iranian government
middle_east.title: Tehran official statement

middle_east.text: We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily — there's not a thing they can do about it.
middle_east.speaker: Donald Trump
middle_east.title: President of the United States

observers.description: North Korea reaffirmed support for Russia's war in Ukraine as foreign ministers met in Moscow. Speculation mounts over a possible Kim Jong Un visit to the Kremlin. Pyongyang continues to supply materiel and personnel to Russian forces.

The US is actively engaged in war against Iran and continues to support Ukraine with military aid. Defense Secretary Hegseth has requested additional congressional funding for the Iran war. The US and Ukraine also signed a landmark drone cooperation agreement.

Germany and France are reported to be conducting their first joint nuclear drills described as strategic coordination, in a move interpreted as directed against Russia. Germany continues broader European defense buildup.
France joined Germany in joint nuclear drills as part of European strategic coordination. France continues to support Ukraine diplomatically and militarily within EU frameworks.

Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with President Trump at the White House in the first such visit in nearly two decades, seeking US support for Israeli withdrawal. Lebanon and Israel have resumed diplomatic relations under US auspices, though southern populations remain skeptical.

Pakistan is serving as a mediator in the US-Iran conflict, hosting talks between a senior Iranian official and other parties aimed at reviving a collapsed ceasefire. Pakistan's role as mediator is a significant diplomatic development.

Saudi Arabia faces a direct Houthi maritime blockade of its ports, adding to its exposure from the broader US-Iran war. Riyadh is navigating extreme pressure between US partnership and Iranian proxy threats, while Gulf business models are disrupted by the ongoing conflict.

Iran resumed offensive operations two weeks after its war with the US restarted, launching missile and drone waves at Bahrain and Jordan. Tehran is rumored to be preparing a major retaliatory strike package. Iraqi PM's visit signals Iran is consolidating regional diplomatic support.

Israel is co-belligerent with the US in attacks on Iran per Al Jazeera casualty tracker data. Netanyahu's planned visit to New York is generating legal controversy over ICC warrant enforcement. Lebanon-Israel diplomatic relations have resumed under US auspices while southern Lebanon violence continues.

Iraq's PM Ali al-Zaidi made a first official visit to Iran on 2026-07-23 to deepen bilateral ties amid the active US-Iran war. Baghdad is attempting to balance its US security relationship against deepening Iranian alignment. The visit signals Iraqi tilt toward Tehran as regional war pressures intensify.

Chinese military researchers published restricted concepts in March 2025 on rapidly seizing Taipei via cognitive warfare per War on the Rocks analysis. No direct involvement in active conflicts reported today. Beijing is closely watching NATO drone-warfare capability development highlighted by Gen. Grynkewich.

financial.notes: Brent crude surged to approximately $94/barrel — its highest level in over a month — as Houthi threats created a simultaneous two-chokepoint problem blocking both Bab el-Mandeb and reducing Hormuz transits, while Trump's threat of Iranian infrastructure strikes rattled energy markets.

update_log.text: Trump threatens imminent heavy strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site, stating 'there's not a thing they can do about it.'
Houthis deploy missiles and drones against ships in southern Red Sea; tankers making sharp U-turns as Bab el-Mandeb blockade takes effect.
Brent crude hits approximately $94/barrel amid dual Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz disruption fears.
Iraq's PM makes first official visit to Iran, deepening Baghdad-Tehran ties amid active US-Iran conflict.
Russia issues arrest warrant for Ukraine's newly appointed Commander-in-Chief Mykhailo Drapatyi immediately after his appointment.
Zelenskyy confirms dismissal of Defense Minister Fedorov and Gen. Syrskyi; Drapatyi named new Commander-in-Chief.
Iran steps up missile and drone attacks on Bahrain and Jordan, two weeks after resumption of active war with US.
Houthis declare full naval blockade of Saudi Arabia and vow 'eye for an eye' after strikes on Sana'a Airport.
North Korea reaffirms military support for Russia; Kim Jong Un visit to Moscow increasingly speculated.
Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones at Moscow region overnight, wounding two and damaging multiple facilities.
Zelenskyy ousts Chief of General Staff one day after replacing top commander Syrsky, completing sweeping military leadership shakeup.
Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi ports; EU naval group raises threat level for Red Sea.
Brent crude exceeds $95/barrel; Indian Sensex falls 715 points on Middle East escalation fears.
US and Ukraine sign bilateral drone cooperation agreement; Ukrainian combat drones to be exported to US for military testing.
Iran steps up attacks; Bahrain and Jordan intercept two waves of missiles and drones.
New Ukrainian top commander Drapatyi says he is tasked with stepping up counteroffensive actions.
Ukraine strikes Wildberries warehouses in Russia for the third time in one week as long-range campaign expands.
Al Jazeera casualty tracker confirms ~3,468 dead in Iran, ~3,696 in Lebanon, ~28 in Gulf states from US-Israel attacks.
Kremlin rules out returning Sumy and Kharkiv border areas in any peace settlement.
Ukraine employed FP-5 Flamingo missiles with salvaged engines to strike deep into Russian territory for the first time.
North Korea reaffirms support for Russia as foreign ministers meet in Moscow; Kim visit to Kremlin speculated.
Ukraine launched ~400 drones at Moscow region overnight, wounding two and damaging several facilities.
